interpretation: VPS37C encodes a protein that is a component of the ESCRT-I complex, which is crucial for vesicular trafficking and the sorting of ubiquitinated proteins into multivesicular bodies. This process is essential for maintaining cellular homeostasis and regulating cell growth and differentiation. While there is no direct evidence linking VPS37C to Steroid-Induced Osteonecrosis of the Femoral Head (SONFH), its role in cellular processes may suggest a potential involvement in the pathophysiology of SONFH, particularly in the context of steroid-induced cellular stress and apoptosis. The evidence is primarily inferred from its biological function and the lack of specific studies connecting it to SONFH. Therefore, the clinical relevance of VPS37C as a biomarker for early detection of SONFH remains weak, as there is no strong evidence supporting its direct involvement in this condition.
relevance_summary: VPS37C may play a role in cellular processes relevant to Steroid-Induced Osteonecrosis of the Femoral Head, but lacks direct evidence linking it to the disease.

result.title: VPS37C (A5D8V6)
result.text:
Protein: Vacuolar protein sorting-associated protein 37C
Function: Component of the ESCRT-I complex, a regulator of vesicular trafficking process. Required for the sorting of endocytic ubiquitinated cargos into multivesicular bodies. May be involved in cell growth and differentiation

result.title: VPS37A
result.text:
Vacuolar protein sorting 37 homolog A (S. cerevisiae) is a protein in humans that is encoded by the VPS37A gene. It is a member of the endosomal sorting complex required for transport (ESCRT) system.

Clinical significance
A missense mutation (K382N) in VPS37A protein has been shown to cause complex hereditary spastic paraparesis (cHSP).

References


== Further reading ==

result.title: VPS28
result.text:
Vacuolar protein sorting-associated protein 28 homolog is a protein that in humans is encoded by the VPS28 gene.

Function
This gene encodes a protein involved in endosomal sorting  of cell surface receptors via a multivesicular body/late endosome pathway. The encoded protein is one of the three subunits of the ESCRT-I complex (endosomal complexes required for transport) involved in the sorting of ubiquitinated protein. The two other subunits of ESCRT-I are vacuolar protein sorting 23 (VPS23), also known as tumor susceptibility gene 101 (TSG101), and vacuolar protein sorting 37 (VPS37). Two alternative transcripts encoding different isoforms have been described. Additional alternative transcripts may exist but the proteins encoded by these transcripts have not been verified experimentally.

Interactions
VPS28 has been shown to interact with TSG101.

References


== Further reading ==

result.title: ABI2
result.text:
Abl interactor 2 also known as Abelson interactor 2 (Abi-2) is a protein that in humans is encoded by the ABI2 gene.

Interactions
ABI2 has been shown to interact with  ABL1, ADAM19, and TRIM32.
